footer.tagline: Independently publishable, Puck-native React component packages — composed into a real visual editor, canvas studio, and export pipeline. This demo validates every package end to end.
footer.bottom: Built with Next.js, Puck, and the @anvilkit/* packages · Styled after the DESIGN.md reference.
meta.home.title: AnvilKit — Puck-native component studio
meta.home.description: Independently publishable, Puck-native React component packages, composed into a real visual editor, canvas studio, AI copilot, and export pipeline.
meta.editor.title: Editor — AnvilKit
meta.editor.description: Every interactive surface of the AnvilKit editor in one place: the visual builder, server render, canvas studio, live collaboration, AI copilot, and the export pipeline.
meta.about.title: About — AnvilKit
meta.about.description: AnvilKit is a monorepo of independently publishable, Puck-native React component packages, plus the Studio shell, IR, and plugin ecosystem that compose them.
meta.root.title: Anvilkit Components Demo
meta.root.description: Puck-native validation surface for the Anvilkit components workspace.
home.hero.lede: Independently publishable React components, composed into a real visual editor, a free-form canvas, an AI copilot, live collaboration, and a clean export pipeline.
home.demo.title: Edit props, render live
home.demo.lede: This is the editor's core loop in miniature: change a block's serializable props on the left and watch the render update on the right. The generated snippet is real, runnable usage.
home.howto.title: From install to published page
home.howto.lede: Four steps take you from an empty project to a published, exportable page built entirely from @anvilkit/* packages.
home.features.title: Everything a page builder needs
home.features.lede: The studio shell, plugins, and packages cover the full authoring lifecycle — editing, AI, collaboration, canvas, and export.
home.feature.puckNative.title: Puck-native
home.feature.puckNative.body: Every package exports a Puck componentConfig — fields, defaultProps, render, metadata — so it drops straight into the editor.
home.feature.independent.title: Independent packages
home.feature.independent.body: No umbrella bundle. Install only the @anvilkit/* blocks you need; each is versioned and published on its own.
home.feature.ai.title: AI Copilot
home.feature.ai.body: Generate whole pages or regenerate a single section from a prompt, wired through the @anvilkit/plugin-ai-copilot pipeline.
home.feature.collab.title: Real-time collaboration
home.feature.collab.body: A one-line managed transport adds shared editing, live cursors, and presence over Yjs — no backend wiring required.
home.feature.canvas.title: Canvas studio
home.feature.canvas.body: A Konva-powered free-form canvas with geometry, snapping, alignment, brand kits, and accessible keyboard tools.
home.feature.export.title: Export pipeline
home.feature.export.body: Publish to a Headless Page IR, then export clean HTML, React/TSX, or JSON with resolved assets.
home.feature.tokens.title: Design system tokens
home.feature.tokens.body: Token-bound field renderers plus off-token and WCAG-AA contrast validators keep every page on-brand.
home.feature.i18n.title: Built-in i18n
home.feature.i18n.body: Components and Studio ship en/zh/ja/ko catalogs with a config-centric locale switch baked into the shell.
home.step.install.title: Install the packages
home.step.install.body: Add the core runtime and just the blocks you want. Each is an independent @anvilkit/* package.
home.step.compose.title: Compose a Puck config
home.step.compose.body: Each package exports a componentConfig. Assemble them into one consumer-owned Puck Config.
home.step.mount.title: Mount the Studio
home.step.mount.body: Render <Studio> from @anvilkit/core with your config and data. Plugins extend it without forking.
home.step.publish.title: Publish & export
home.step.publish.body: Publish to the Headless Page IR, then export production HTML, React, or JSON with resolved assets.
home.cta.title: Ready to build a page?
home.cta.lede: Jump into the editor hub for every interactive surface, or read the docs for the full package reference.
editorHub.intro.title: Every editor surface, in one place
editorHub.intro.lede: The visual builder, server render, canvas studio, live collaboration, the AI copilot, and the export pipeline — each wired to the same consumer-owned Puck config. Try the live editor below, then dive into any surface.
editorHub.embed.title: The real editor, right here
editorHub.embed.ledeBefore: This is a live, embedded instance of the Studio editor — the same route as
editorHub.embed.ledeAfter: . Drag blocks, edit props, and open the left-rail plugins. It loads lazily to keep this page fast.
editorHub.surfaces.title: Routes you can open now
editorHub.surfaces.lede: Each surface renders the same shared component config from a different angle.
editorHub.surface.visual.title: Visual editor
editorHub.surface.visual.body: The full Studio shell — drag blocks in, edit their props in the inspector, manage pages, and publish.
editorHub.surface.render.title: Server render
editorHub.surface.render.body: The RSC-safe read view of the same Puck payload, shipping zero editor JavaScript to the client.
editorHub.surface.canvas.title: Canvas studio
editorHub.surface.canvas.body: A free-form Konva canvas: shapes, snapping, alignment, brand kits, and accessible keyboard tools.
editorHub.surface.collab.title: Live collaboration
editorHub.surface.collab.body: Shared editing with live remote cursors and presence over Yjs — the managed-transport one-liner.
editorHub.surface.navbar.title: Navbar playground
editorHub.surface.navbar.body: Tune the @anvilkit/navbar props live — logo modes, links, CTA variants — and copy the generated JSX.
editorHub.surface.hero.title: Hero playground
editorHub.surface.hero.body: Render the @anvilkit/hero package with its real exported defaults and styles.
editorHub.plugins.title: Plugins that extend the Studio
editorHub.plugins.lede: These ship as @anvilkit plugins and are wired into the demo editor. Open the visual editor and find each in the header or left rail.
editorHub.plugin.ai.title: AI Copilot
editorHub.plugin.ai.body: Generate a whole page or regenerate a single section from a prompt. Open the AI Copilot tab in the left rail.
editorHub.plugin.export.title: Export pipeline
editorHub.plugin.export.body: Publish to the Headless Page IR, then download production HTML, React/TSX, or JSON with resolved assets.
editorHub.plugin.assets.title: Asset manager
editorHub.plugin.assets.body: Upload images, browse folders, and (with a key) pull from Unsplash — all resolvable at export time.
editorHub.plugin.history.title: Version history
editorHub.plugin.history.body: Snapshot the document as you edit and restore any earlier state from the history panel.
editorHub.plugin.design.title: Design system
editorHub.plugin.design.body: Token-bound field renderers with off-token and WCAG-AA contrast validators keep pages on-brand.
editorHub.plugin.seo.title: Page SEO
editorHub.plugin.seo.body: Edit canonical URL, Open Graph image, description, and robots directives on the page model.
about.intro.title: Pages built from packages you can publish
about.intro.lede: AnvilKit is a monorepo of independently publishable, Puck-native React components — and the Studio shell, IR, and plugins that compose them into a complete authoring experience.
about.principles.title: A publishing model, not a kitchen sink
about.principles.lede: The whole project is organised around one idea: a component is a package, and a page is serializable data.
about.principle.1: Each component is its own npm package under @anvilkit/* — no umbrella bundle, so consumers install only what they need.
about.principle.2: Every package honours the Puck contract: componentConfig, defaultProps, fields, and metadata, ready for both direct rendering and the editor.
about.principle.3: Render components accept only serializable props, so a page is just data that round-trips through Puck, the IR, and export.
about.principle.4: Components are built with Rslib to ship CJS, ESM, and type definitions, and are versioned independently with Changesets.
about.stack.title: What powers the studio
about.stack.lede: A small set of foundational packages, a growing catalog of components, and a plugin layer that extends the editor without forking it.
about.stack.core.body: The runtime, plugin engine, and the <Studio> editor shell that hosts Puck.
about.stack.ir.body: The Headless Page IR and the transforms that turn Puck data into portable output.
about.stack.schema.body: AI-friendly schema derivation that powers the copilot and validation.
about.stack.validator.body: A Puck Config validator that guards the editor and publish path.
about.stack.components.name: Component packages
about.stack.components.body: Hero, Navbar, Pricing, Bento Grid, Statistics, Blog List, and more — each publishable on its own.
about.stack.plugins.name: Plugin ecosystem
about.stack.plugins.body: AI copilot, asset manager, canvas studio, collaboration, design system, version history, and export plugins.
about.cta.title: See it for yourself
about.cta.lede: Open the editor hub to try every surface, or jump straight into the visual builder.
mini.field.headline: Headline
mini.default.headline: Compose pages from real packages.
mini.default.body: Every block is an independently published @anvilkit/* package. Edit its props here — the preview re-renders live.
